content: Just as biological/chemical agents produce cascading effects by acting on shared substrates in natural systems, AI multi-agent active learning systems can leverage a scalable shared retention memory architecture to let individual agents leave persistent, action-altering traces that shape the learning behavior of the entire collective. This creates a natural, emergent active sampling dynamic that mimics how biological agents co-evolve by responding to shared environmental changes left by prior activity.
source_topics: Scalable shared memory architecture for multi-agent active learning
trigger_topic: Scalable shared memory architecture for multi-agent active learning
